options: It is transported as starch directly through the xylem tissue.
It is converted into soluble sugars and transported through the phloem tissue.
It diffuses through the stomata and travels through the air to other plant parts.
It is broken down into water and $CO_2$ and absorbed by the roots.
questionText: How is stored carbohydrate (starch) made available to other parts of a plant when needed for growth?
